caption: Axial graphic depicts pathology of classic chronic subdural hematoma (SDH) with the formation of thin internal , thicker external membranes. Note the stretched bridging veins crossing the SDH, top-to-bottom gradation of fluid from serosanguinous to more proteinaceous contents.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

caption: Axial NECT depicts chronic SDH as a crescentic, hypodense extraaxial fluid collection over the left hemisphere. Stretched cortical veins are faintly visible as they cross the SDH.

caption: Axial graphic depicts "mixed" SDH. Numerous loculations that contain hemorrhages of multiple ages are present. Note the fluid-fluid levels with mixed acute, subacute blood . Rehemorrhage into preexisting subacute &/or chronic SDH is common.

caption: More cephalad NECT in the same case shows margins of the loculated chronic SDHs are heavily calcified . This unusual appearance is sometimes termed "armored brain."

caption: This case illustrates the concept of devascularizing membranes of chronic SDHs by embolizing the middle meningeal arteries. This 78-year-old man had recurrent chronic SDHs, shown on this NECT as mixed-density SDHs .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

caption: Lateral DSA of the selective left external carotid artery in the same patient shows the normal middle meningeal artery . The superficial temporal and maxillary arteries are also opacified.

caption: DSA of the left external carotid artery in the same patient after embolization of the middle meningeal artery (MMA) shows the superficial temporal and maxillary arteries. The MMA has been occluded and is not opacified.

caption: Axial NECT in the same patient 2 weeks after the left MMA embolization shows the left mixed a/cSDH has become progressively more hypodense. The right cSDH is also slightly more hypodense compared to the initial NECT.

caption: Axial NECT 6 weeks after the left MMA embolization shows the left cSDH continues to decrease in density and is slightly smaller. The right chronic SDH has almost completely resolved.

caption: Axial NECT 5 months later shows both chronic SDHs have almost completely resorbed, and there is no evidence for recurrent hemorrhage. One year after MMA embolization, the patient is normal and has had no recurrence of SDHs.
